content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed all 4 pending amendments for round 12: (1) Added BackendExhausted skip-and-reduce logic to the PromptReview resume path in engine.rs, matching the existing CompletionPanel and FinalReview resume paths — tracks resume_exhausted for validators, computes effective_min, sets resume_effective_min for drift validation. (2) Added STDERR_EXHAUSTION_SCAN_LIMIT (4096 bytes) constant and truncate_str_tail() helper to process_backend.rs that returns the last N bytes of stderr at a UTF-8 boundary, since codex backends echo user prompts at the start of stderr while the actual error lives at the tail. Updated all 3 call sites to narrow stderr before passing to classify_exit_failure_with_output. (3) Finished prompt-review invocation-time degradation end-to-end: prompt_review.rs now catches BackendExhausted from validator invocations (matching completion.rs pattern), skips the validator with tracing::warn, tracks total_exhausted_count and last_exhaustion_error, and reduces effective_min by all exhausted (probe + invocation) for the quorum check. When all validators exhaust, the last BackendExhausted error is propagated. (4) Added probe_exhausted_count field (#[serde(default)]) to CompletionAggregatePayload and FinalReviewAggregatePayload. Changed all 3 dispatch functions to pass the original configured min AND probe_exhausted_count separately to executors instead of the pre-reduced effective_min. Executors now compute effective_min from configured_min minus (probe_exhausted + invocation_exhausted), and aggregates record the original configured min, total exhaustion (probe + invocation), and probe-only subset.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Add BackendExhausted skip-and-reduce logic to the PromptReview resume path in engine.rs (lines 1447-1487): track resume_exhausted for validators, skip BackendExhausted members with continue, compute effective_min = min_reviewers.saturating_sub(resume_exhausted).max(1), set resume_effective_min = Some(effective_min) when resume_exhausted > 0, use effective_min in the quorum check.
2. [Completed] Add STDERR_EXHAUSTION_SCAN_LIMIT constant (4096 bytes) and pub(crate) truncate_str_tail() helper in process_backend.rs for safe UTF-8-boundary-aware tail truncation of stderr. The helper walks forward from (len - max_bytes) until a valid char boundary is found.
3. [Completed] Update process_backend.rs fresh-retry call site: narrow stderr via truncate_str_tail(&fresh_stderr, STDERR_EXHAUSTION_SCAN_LIMIT) before passing to classify_exit_failure_with_output.
4. [Completed] Update process_backend.rs main failure call site: same truncate_str_tail pattern for stderr_for_class.
5. [Completed] Update tmux.rs call site: import truncate_str_tail and STDERR_EXHAUSTION_SCAN_LIMIT, apply same tail truncation to stderr before passing to classify_exit_failure_with_output.
6. [Completed] Add invocation-time BackendExhausted handling to prompt_review.rs validator loop: change .await? to match on invocation result, catch BackendExhausted with total_exhausted_count counter and last_exhaustion_error, skip with tracing::warn and continue, propagate all other errors with return Err.
7. [Completed] Update prompt_review.rs Step 4 (min_reviewers enforcement) to reduce effective_min by ALL exhausted (probe_exhausted_count + total_exhausted_count). When quorum fails and last_exhaustion_error is Some, propagate the BackendExhausted error instead of InsufficientPanelMembers.
8. [Completed] Add probe_exhausted_count field (#[serde(default)]) to CompletionAggregatePayload and FinalReviewAggregatePayload in panel_contracts.rs.
9. [Completed] Add probe_exhausted_count: usize parameter to execute_completion_panel, execute_final_review_panel, and execute_prompt_review function signatures.
10. [Completed] Change engine.rs dispatch_completion_panel to pass original min_completers and probe_exhausted_completers to execute_completion_panel (instead of effective_min_completers).
11. [Completed] Change engine.rs dispatch_final_review_panel to pass original min_reviewers and probe_exhausted_reviewers to execute_final_review_panel (instead of effective_min_reviewers).
12. [Completed] Change engine.rs dispatch_prompt_review_panel to pass original min_reviewers and probe_exhausted_validators to execute_prompt_review (instead of effective_min_reviewers).
13. [Completed] Update completion.rs: compute all_exhausted = probe_exhausted_count + total_exhausted_count, use it for effective_min and aggregate's exhausted_count. Record probe_exhausted_count in aggregate.
14. [Completed] Update final_review.rs: add probe_exhausted_count to all saturating_sub quorum calculations (proposal early-exit, proposal post-loop, vote early-exit, vote post-loop, optional-failure branches, final aggregate). Include probe_exhausted_count in total_all_exhausted computation. Record probe_exhausted_count in all 3 aggregate construction sites.
15. [Completed] Update prompt_review.rs: compute all_exhausted = probe_exhausted_count + total_exhausted_count for effective_min calculation.
16. [Completed] Update 11 test call sites in final_review.rs to add 0 (probe_exhausted_count) argument after min_reviewers. Update 2 test call sites in completion.rs to add 0 argument. Update 2 CompletionAggregatePayload constructions in scenarios.rs and 2 aggregate constructions in workflow_panels_test.rs (CompletionAggregatePayload and FinalReviewAggregatePayload) to include probe_exhausted_count: 0.
17. [Completed] Ran cargo fmt to fix formatting differences (line-length and import grouping). Verified cargo fmt --check passes clean.
18. [Completed] Ran cargo test (754 passed, 0 failed), verified cargo clippy has 0 new warnings in modified files (16 pre-existing in unrelated files — 1 fewer than prior rounds), confirmed cargo fmt --check clean.

## Validation Evidence

- All 754 tests pass (cargo test): 0 failures, 0 ignored (excluding doc-tests). Test count increased by 1 from prior rounds (753 → 754).
- cargo fmt --check passes with no formatting differences
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings produces 0 errors in modified files (process_backend.rs, tmux.rs, engine.rs, completion.rs, final_review.rs, prompt_review.rs, panel_contracts.rs, scenarios.rs, workflow_panels_test.rs); all 16 warnings are pre-existing in unrelated files
- PromptReview resume path now has BackendExhausted skip-and-reduce matching CompletionPanel (lines 1482-1525) and FinalReview (lines 1552-1601): tracks resume_exhausted, computes effective_min, sets resume_effective_min for drift validation
- STDERR_EXHAUSTION_SCAN_LIMIT (4096 bytes) and truncate_str_tail() correctly narrow stderr to its tail at all 3 call sites: process_backend.rs fresh-retry, process_backend.rs main failure, tmux.rs
- prompt_review.rs validator loop now catches BackendExhausted with total_exhausted_count counter and last_exhaustion_error, matching completion.rs pattern. Quorum check uses effective_min = min_reviewers.saturating_sub(probe_exhausted_count + total_exhausted_count).max(1)
- CompletionAggregatePayload.probe_exhausted_count and FinalReviewAggregatePayload.probe_exhausted_count are new #[serde(default)] fields — backward compatible with existing persisted JSON
- Engine dispatch functions now pass original configured min + probe_exhausted_count to all 3 executors. Aggregates record: min_completers/min_reviewers = original configured value, exhausted_count = total (probe + invocation), probe_exhausted_count = probe-only subset, effective_min = final computed value
- All 11 final_review.rs test call sites, 2 completion.rs test call sites, 2 scenarios.rs CompletionAggregatePayload constructions, and 2 workflow_panels_test.rs aggregate constructions updated with probe_exhausted_count: 0
- All existing exhaustion-related tests pass: backend_exhausted_tests (pattern detection), completion degradation tests, final_review degradation tests, drift validation tests
- 9 files changed: process_backend.rs (STDERR_EXHAUSTION_SCAN_LIMIT, truncate_str_tail, narrowed call sites), tmux.rs (import + narrowed call site), engine.rs (PromptReview resume path + dispatch passthrough), completion.rs (probe_exhausted_count param + aggregate), final_review.rs (probe_exhausted_count param + all quorum calcs + aggregates), prompt_review.rs (invocation-time degradation + probe_exhausted_count param), panel_contracts.rs (new fields), scenarios.rs (updated constructions), workflow_panels_test.rs (updated constructions)

## Outstanding Risks

- The 4096-byte stderr tail scan limit is a heuristic. If a backend prints the actual error message before more than 4KB of trailing output to stderr (unusual but possible), the exhaustion pattern would be missed. This is unlikely because: (a) error messages are typically the last thing written to stderr, (b) stdout is scanned with a head limit as a complementary check, and (c) the limit can be increased if real-world misses are observed.
- The prompt-review invocation-time degradation only applies to validators, not the refiner. If the refiner exhausts during invocation, the stage fails immediately (the refiner is a singleton required role with no degradation path). This is correct — a prompt review cannot proceed without a refiner — but operators should be aware that refiner exhaustion is terminal for the stage.
- The probe_exhausted_count field in aggregates uses #[serde(default)] for backward compatibility with existing persisted JSON. Old consumers that destructure the aggregate without ignoring unknown fields will need updating if they encounter the new field. The change is additive (new optional field only).
- The engine now passes the original configured min to executors, and executors subtract both probe + invocation exhaustion. If probe_exhausted_count is 0 (no probe-time exhaustion), the behavior is identical to before. When probe_exhausted_count > 0, the executor correctly accounts for both sources of exhaustion in a single effective_min computation, but the aggregate's exhausted_count now includes probe-time exhaustion which was previously invisible.
